content.problemStatement:
Chef and Chefina are shopping for ingredients.

The shop has NNN ingredients, arranged in a row from left to right. The iii-th ingredient has a weight of WiW_iWi​ units.

Each ingredient has the same cost, that being CCC coins.

Chef's bag can carry a weight of upto MMM units.

Chef will shop greedily; that is, he'll perform the following process:


For each iii from 111 to NNN in order, if it's possible to add the iii-th ingredient to his bag without the total weight exceeding MMM, he will do so.
If it's not possible, he'll **immediately stop shopping**, and not look at further ingredients.
Chefina doesn't want Chef to spend too much money, so she tries to help him a bit.

Before Chef starts his process, Chefina can do the following as many times as she likes:


Choose two indices iii and jjj (1≤i<j≤N1 \leq i \lt j \leq N1≤i<j≤N), and swap the ingredients at these indices.

That is, swap WiW_iWi​ and WjW_jWj​.
Each swap incurs a cost of SSS coins.

If Chefina chooses her swaps optimally, find the **minimum total** number of coins that Chef and Chefina need to pay on their shopping trip.

Note that if Chefina performs xxx swaps, and Chef then puts yyy ingredients into his bag following his greedy process, the total cost is x⋅S+y⋅Cx\cdot S + y\cdot Cx⋅S+y⋅C coins.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains four space-separated integers NNN, CCC, SSS and MMM — the number of ingredients, the cost of each ingredient, the cost of a swap, and the capacity of Chef's bag, respectively.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers W1,W2,…,WNW_1, W_2, \ldots, W_NW1​,W2​,…,WN​, denoting the weights of the ingredients.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line one integer: the minimum total number of coins that can be spent.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: If Chefina does not perform any swaps, Chef will buy the first 333 items and then his bag will become full. The total cost is 0⋅9+3⋅10=300\cdot 9 + 3\cdot 10 = 300⋅9+3⋅10=30 coins.
But, if Chefina swaps the 3rd3^{rd}3rd item with the 5th5^{th}5th item, Chef will only be able to buy the first 222 items, with the total cost being 1⋅9+2⋅10=291\cdot 9 + 2\cdot 10 = 291⋅9+2⋅10=29 coins. It can be shown that this is optimal.
Test case 222: Chef has enough capacity in his bag to hold all items, so he ends up spending 0⋅8+2⋅6=120\cdot 8 + 2\cdot 6 = 120⋅8+2⋅6=12 coins.
Test case 333: Chefina swaps 3rd3^{rd}3rd item with 4th4^{th}4th item, and 1⋅7+2⋅10=271\cdot 7 + 2\cdot 10 = 271⋅7+2⋅10=27 coins will be spent.
